my-workspace/server/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target configuration with release configuration in maven-compiler-plugin for Java 21 compatibility.
my-workspace/server/pom.xml.why_exists: The source and target options are deprecated in newer Java versions; using release is required for proper cross-compilation and module path handling in Java 21.

my-workspace/server/src/main/java/com/my.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
my-workspace/server/src/main/java/com/my.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial on serialVersionUID, the code may fail to compile or trigger warnings/errors depending on the compiler configuration (e.g., --enable-preview or specific linting rules enabled in the migration recipe).

my-workspace/server/src/main/java/com/my.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
my-workspace/server/src/main/java/com/my.why_exists: Compilation failure: The @Serial annotation is required on serialization fields (like serialVersionUID) in Java 14+ when the module system or strict serialization checks are enabled, or to satisfy modern linters/compilers enforcing JEP 416.

my-workspace/server/src/main/java/com/my.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
